expected_calibration_error.exempt_reason: single populated confidence bin at this n — there is nothing to bin, so the raw gap is reported without a CI (the two-figure n+CI exemption)

miss_rate.exempt_reason: the judge's real-draft miss rate — too few naturally-wrong drafts to put a CI on; the injected conformance-flip detection rate is the trustworthy upper bound reported instead

judge.rationale_coherence_note: Conformance flip is a pure mutation — no rationale regeneration. The judge rubric grades conformance from the finding and cited SC only and does not read the draft's remediation prose, so a flipped verdict is coherent to the judge (not a self-contradictory strawman); no LLM re-authorship was introduced, avoiding that authorship bias entirely.

tier_b.clean_vs_noisy_note: page-a-title (focal document-title [failed]): clean=correct, noisy=correct, 0 noise-region FP | page-b-label (focal label [passed]): clean=wrong, noisy=wrong, 1 noise-region FP. Focal verdicts changed under noise: 0/2; total noise-region false positives: 1. The clean − noisy delta is the cost of real-page noise, reported as illustration (n=2, no CI).
tier_b.method_and_limits: Each ACT snippet is embedded INTACT as the focal case, preserving its local context; the noise is built from ACT passed snippets plus trivially-descriptive authored chrome (clean by construction), so a noise-region flag citing a tested property counts as a false positive while a flag citing an unrelated SC is excluded. Scored exactly like the bare ACT cases (deterministic vs gold), but n=2 is illustrative, not a rate — no confidence interval attaches to two points, and the methodology is preliminary.

not_measured.what: Expert-minutes-per-finding, and whether a drafted remediation is genuinely useful to an implementer
not_measured.why: both need a real accessibility specialist's time — the one resource unavailable; the benchmark checks only alignment with the canonical technique (direction), which is a proxy, not efficacy.

not_measured.what: Recall / missed findings — how much the system failed to raise
not_measured.why: a Finding exists only when axe emits something; what axe cannot see (reading order, motion) is invisible to the pipeline too, so total miss volume cannot be measured here.
raised via passes[] but not validatable — the ACT filename leaks the answer, so a DOM-only pipeline would score filename-matching, not image-text-correspondence judgment; needs a multimodal drafter.
judgment-item scores in production are the judge's, so no judgment-item number can be trusted beyond judge_kappa — the judge's accuracy is their upper bound.

scorecard.notes: FP over the 27 non-trivial true negatives (dropping the passed honest-misses that mint no finding) = 13/27 = 0.481; the headline FP uses all true negatives. partially_supports scored as CLEAN instead of FLAGS → recall 17/23 = 0.739, FP 11/30 = 0.367. Construct-validity: among failed cases whose cited SC intersects the ACT SC (n=12), conformance-correct on 11 (0.917). not_applicable drafts (n=0) are CLEAN under the primary collapse but reported separately as abstained_n, never folded silently. remediation_technique_match is not wired (ACT G/F technique metadata is not vendored) — see the not-measured list.
